title: Repeater workstation
description: Configure behavior = REPEATER for change-triggered looping and rejection reloop semantics without cron or poller scheduling.
openingSummary: REPEATER is the WorkstationKind scheduling behavior for change-triggered loops. Set behavior to REPEATER when a step should reloop on upstream change or rejection routing, without cron schedules or long-lived poller ingress.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Author a workstation with behavior REPEATER. Keep type on the runtime axis for the execution family you need. Bind a worker when the type requires one. Wire inputs plus outputs, onContinue, onRejection, and onFailure so rejection and continue routes can re-enter the loop. Do not set cron on REPEATER. Choose STANDARD for fire-once dispatch, CRON for schedules, or POLLER for long-lived poller ingress. REPEATER is not CRON — it excludes the cron field — and it is not STANDARD or POLLER scheduling.

schemaReference.title: Schema reference
schemaReference.body: The embed below shows the live Workstation base definition with REPEATER overlay applicability. REPEATER has no selected exclusive fields. Excluded fields belong to other WorkstationType families or to CRON scheduling and must not appear on this behavior. Open the full Factory schema reference for exhaustive field lookup rather than copying contract text into this page.

examples.title: Examples
examples.body: Use the minimal valid example when starting a REPEATER workstation. The misuse example shows cron on REPEATER — cron belongs on behavior CRON and is excluded from REPEATER.

links.companionIntro: REPEATER is compatible with every published WorkstationType. Bind a matching Worker for worker-backed types; LOGICAL_MOVE stays worker-free.
links.misuseNote: cron is a CRON scheduling field. REPEATER excludes it. Use behavior CRON when the step must run on a schedule.
